new_findings.title: S3 plan_invalid JSON parse error — steps_json truncation
new_findings.description: S3 (plan_summary_across_n_files) failed with plan_invalid: steps_json JSON parse error at char 660. The LLM-generated steps_json was truncated mid-JSON, causing the plan tool to reject it. The router then fell back to spawn_agent instead of retrying plan. This caused plan_emitted=0 (required >=1), producing verdict R.
new_findings.observation: events: tool_returned for plan shows error.kind=plan_invalid, error.message contains 'Expecting comma delimiter'. No plan_emitted, no plan_step_started events.
new_findings.hypothesis: LLM generating steps_json hit an output token limit or malformed the JSON for a 3-step plan. The input prompt uses full doc paths (post-B54 fix) so path issue is not the cause.

new_findings.title: search_actions not called in any plan_mode scenario
new_findings.description: Zero search_actions calls observed across all 5 scenarios. Plan steps used reyn_src_read + recall exclusively for document retrieval. This is consistent with B56 W6 findings.
new_findings.observation: tool_called events across all 5 scenarios: plan, reyn_src_read, recall only. search_actions count=0.
